title: French 'libertines' rapes case: From livestreams to camp sites, where the sexual crimes took place
subhead: Christophe B. and many other men he recruited are accused of raping women, with these sexual assaults broadcast online and in public. Responsibility is now being called into question. This is the final part of our investigation into the French 'libertines' rapes case.

title: Between China and the US, a new diplomacy of detainees emerges
Andy Burnham enters Downing Street and appoints former defense minister John Healey as chancellor of the Exchequer
Tate brothers' lawyer says they will fight UK extradition on sex crime charges
subhead: US authorities detained self-professed misogynist Andrew Tate and his younger brother Tristan on Saturday as UK prosecutors announced new charges of rape, sex trafficking and assault. In Romania, the Tates face allegations of trafficking minors, sexual intercourse with a minor and money laundering.

title: In southern Lebanon, at the Secours populaire hospital in Nabatieh, caregivers strive to overcome the traumas of war
subhead: Doctors Without Borders provides individual counseling and group therapy sessions to professionals who have endured four months of conflict and an ongoing, unstable security situation.

title: Climate change: Why the ECB refuses to create a lower interest rate for green investments
subhead: China's central bank guarantees low-cost funding for green investments. Its European counterpart, although a frontrunner on climate change, currently refuses to use this tool.

title: Cleanliness in Paris: Study reveals 1,412 spots left systematically unclean
subhead: Paris City Hall turned to architects and municipal cleaning workers to identify 1,412 'black spots,' small public spaces that are systematically soiled or clogged with bulky waste, which it aims to reduce to 1,000.

subhead: Le Monde's critics present our selection of exhibitions on painting, contemporary art and photography across France.

title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.

title: In Paris café, terror attack survivors find community: 'People want to tell each other: I am alive'
subhead: After long days attending the 2015 Paris attacks trial, survivors and relatives of victims met in a bar to comfort one another. They have continued to gather every month, joined by victims of other attacks, to share experiences, advice and, above all, laughter and good company.
